I prefer this groovy rhythm that transports me to 90s songs the best era to dance I guess. These days are like full of generic music and they all sound the same. It's sad and hard but music doesn't sound fun anymore and Madonna is always here to show us the good music is still alive. I am very upset with the ageism ppl put on her and she's battling her own canoe completely alone against all odds and still stomping an all prejudice out there that is frankly appalling. Music is definitely one of my favorite Madonna albums and albums in music in general

For this song she worked with producer William Orbit and it's very inspired by the Trip-Hop that was going on in the UK at the time, such as Portishead, Massive Attack, Morcheeba and Sneaker Pimps. That genre has a lot of dark & moody electronic songs like this, with very sultry singing. Check out Massive Attack's album Mezzanine from the same year 1998, like the song Dissolved Girl or their song 6 Underground by Sneaker Pimps. This genre is some of my favorite music of all time, and I'm glad Madonna dipped her toe in it to give us her version.

Do napisania przeboju, zainspirował Madonnę taniec o nazwie Vogue. Ten sposób poruszania się był niezwykle popularny w środowisku gejowskim na przełomie lat 80-tych i 90-tych. Tancerze wykonując wymyślne gesty rękami, ustawiali się w wyszukane pozy i zatrzymywali się, aby przez chwilę wyglądać jak z okładki słynnego magazynu. Piosenka Madonny umożliwiła szerszej publiczności poznanie ruchów wykonywanych w trakcie Vogue. Dzięki temu, wokalistka umocniła swoją pozycję ikony w kręgach homoseksualnych.
Taniec ma być dla Madonny oraz jej słuchaczy formą terapii. Dzięki niemu możliwe staje się zapomnienie o trudach życia i bólu istnienia. Szaleństwo na parkiecie jest pomocne, nawet jeżeli wszystkie inne środki zawiodły. „Wystarczy bowiem użyć wyobraźni” i zanurzyć się w marzeniach, a wszystkie problemy na chwilę znikną. Vogue daje możliwość posmakowania innego życia, pozwala poczuć się gwiazdą.

Yeah Madonna has always had catchy much that many loved and many got pissed off about. my mom graduated high school in 1985. She was a huge fan and I asked her if my grandparents said anything about it and she said they listened to country music and was totally not in the pop world and no, never said anything when my mom would play her records. I think she was 16 when Everybody came out. Grandparents very religious and conservative and never said anything. My grandmother claims she didn't know about her other than the name. Of course I showed her all the videos and songs starting with 80s. Interviews too. My 85 year old grandmother said "yeah I remember hearing that (holiday) and I kept on and she said "she's not hot stuff like she thinks. She's trashy." I laughed and said my mom loved her back then and said my mom told me y'all never said anything. She said "well I don't remember but I'm sure she's right and I didn't because your mom always did what she wanted to." My mom said hookey was a big thing, skipping school. She said it was so easy and also to call in from the gym to the office to check out as if you're the parent. my mom got caught once and she said that my grandmother just kept saying "this makes me so mad you do this." She skipped a lot to go to the mall with her best friend Carol. She said Carol's mom didn't care and then she got with my dad and my mother grandmother didn't care either lol. So my mom and dad skipped school all the time. Got married in 87 and still married to this day. Which is rare. Had me in 94.

content:
People say Masahiro Ito took inspiration from this music video to create the horror ambience of SH2, but for me, it's very clear the resemblances lean more towards SH3, not very much to SH2.

Anyone who remembers the aesthetics of those two games would inmediately think of the SH3 otherworld when seeing the hallway in which Madonna is dancing, and that's because SH2's otherworld has more green colors in it, while in SH3 it has the same reddish tones of said hallway.

Another thing that sticks out to me is Madonna's dress, which is mostly identical to the God's dress depicted in the paintings of the church of SH3, and the fact that at the start of the video, she is holding a baby-like thing, giving me more reason to believe that this video has more to do with the Order's God and Alessa's reincarnation, not really with James' guilt.

Evita and Peron suffer poverty or economic turnmoil in their own childhood. The problem with peronism was the corruption and thrist for power, clientelism, and their neo-fascism that found enemies almost everywhere who didn't supported them, especially in the high terratenient class, the Catholic Church and finally the middle class and the Armed Forces (being Peron a military Colonel before ascending to government power). ***** Dude you're just in denial...you just can't accept the fact that Madonna has always been a trend setter in both entertainment and fashion worlds. As I said you need to read more articles about Madonna. She's the most influential and iconic female recording artist of all time and her influence reigns supreme on today's artists. The very fact that she's doing something different and controversial is the reason why she's popular and inspirational. When you're influential and inspirational, everything about you is worth following. Ever heard of Madonna wannabes trend in the 80s? Young women wanted to be like her. They wanted to follow the direction she led, they wanted to do what she did, they wanted to dress like her, in short they wanted to be like her. Everything about her became a trend on its own whether or not she was conscious about it. When you talk about 80's fashion, first thing that pops into mind is Madonna. She introduced dance pop and popularized vogueing and the rave culture in the 90s. Her ability to constantly reinvent herself and her music has helped to secure her status as an icon, and she has influenced a generation of copycats. Each new Madonna's style is a trend and is used by major designers as inspiration. And now, younger artists want to keep on doing what she has started and pioneered way back when none dared to speak of it, namely standing up for LGBT community, human rights, sex equality, AIDS/HIV, and peace. This trend will continue until these issues have finally become things of the past. Trending at this moment is the fight against age discrimination. Madonna started it and now other artists and celebrities are not only supporting her but also following her footsteps. This trend will also continue until ageism has become a thing of the past.

content:
In 2012, which was seven years ago, I was 16 going on 17. I was struggling from relative poverty, a loss of social status, ostracism because of my sexual orientation, problems at school, and to top it all off, I was caught in the throes of depression. I remember crying in the back of a public bus on the way back from school in early 2012 because it'd been a particularly rough day for me. I then decided to browse through YouTube on my phone to find a song to listen to and distract me from my problems. I was on the home page, and I saw that YouTube had recommended me Madonna's Girl Gone Wild lyric video, which was released in February 2012.

I'd never listened to a Madonna song prior to that because she was from a different generation than mine, so I wasn't familiar with her discography. The aesthetic of the thumbnail drew me in, nonetheless, so I thought I'd give it a try. I didn't know what to expect at first - but then as I listened to it, I became completely, utterly enthralled.

To say that I loved it would be an understatement. I was captivated beyond belief. I loved the beat. I loved the lyrics, which were suggestive, yet not excessively so. I loved the way the song was an unabashed affirmation of her sexuality - it spoke really powerfully to me, as a confused, upset, depressed gay boy that felt like he had no-one in the world - not a shoulder to cry on. I then discovered this music video shortly afterwards, and it was at that very moment that I knew that I'd become a Madonna fan for life. 

MDNA single-handedly got me through some of my worst years in life. It was the first physical album that I'd ever purchased. I listened to and memorized every single song. There were times when things got so bleak for me that I legitimately felt like just ending it all, but then I'd listen to 'Falling Free' to soothe me and put those dark thoughts to rest. Whenever I wanted to mentally escape... when I was desperate to feel a sense of hope for my future... I'd listen to 'Superstar', 'Turn Up The Radio', and 'Give Me All Your Luvin'. Whenever I wanted to feel a sense of confidence in my own sexuality, I'd listen to 'Girl Gone Wild' and 'Beautiful Killer'. Of course, it wasn't just MDNA alone - my discovery of that album served as a gateway to the rest of Madonna's discography, which helped me as well - but MDNA remains the closest to my heart because its release coincided with one of the bleakest phases in my life.

My life has stabilized since then and is now back on track. I still have some unresolved trauma from the past, and I still have my bad days, but they're nowhere as bad as they were a couple of years back. Madonna, thank you for being you. You don't know who I am, for I'm just another face in your sea of fans, but you've impacted my life in ways that are incomprehensible. I regret not being able to go to her MDNA tour or see her live, but that's okay. Maybe one day I'll be able to see her performing live.

To anyone out there who feels like they aren't going to make it... trust me, you will. It really does get better. It may not seem that way right now, but that's only because it can be hard to have a sense of foresight when there are stumbling blocks in your way. It may take a few years, but it will get better. Stay strong, and you do you. Never, ever let anybody bring you down or tell you you aren't worthy.

content: Well with this video specifically I think it's becase the video was posted several times on multiple 'official' accounts, thus cannibalizing and resetting the number of views; I distinctly remember it reaching 50 million somewhere. Altogether the video has probably accumulated somewhere in the range of 100 million views. As for Madonna's music in general, you can blame ageism and anti-Madonna-ism. The entire industry and society refuses to play or listen to any female over 30, be it Madonna or Britney or Katy or even Gaga. The older you get, the more society vehemently opposes listening to your music, if you're a woman. So, Madonna is forbidden from being popular because of her age. Plus, people generally resent Madonna for daring to defy that 'rule', and for her philanthropic work and adoption of orphans in Malawi, and for her success, and for not caving in to all the haters. She's a strong, successful woman who doesn't have to act or live the way people want her to, and for that people resent her. Listening to her music is considered uncool; it's something you do in secret so people don't make fun of you. For those reasons, Madonna doesn't get too many hits in this country.

It's nice to be able to write like that. I enjoy it. However, I've had issues putting music to everything I've written. Published poems. @Cutting Yoko’s bra! Kim Wilde was only a success in Europe though until 1986, when she hit it big with a remake of You Keep Me Hanging On.  And if she bought her clothes at thrift stores than it surprises me.  However, I don't think she has ever influenced Madonna.  Madonna has mentioned all of her influences over the years, even besides Marilyn.  Such as Brigitte Bardot and other famous blondes.

Also, here's a tidbit of information that apparently you know nothing about: At one time before she became really successful, she changed her blonde hair to dark.  She was in Europe and when she came back to the states, her record company told her to change it back to blonde.  

So her image was even changing and evolving then.  We just didn't know it until more recent years.

Btw, Madonna points her influences pacifically to Debra Harry and Chrissie Hyndes of the Pretenders.  If you ever listen to very early unreleased songs, you can definitely hear the sound of Chrissie in her vocals.

content:
Datos interesantes sobre "Frozen" para los jóvenes que apenas están descubriendo el Legado de Madonna :
-“Frozen” es una canción que viene incluida en su séptimo álbum de estudio, “Ray of Light” (1998).
-Fue compuesta por Madonna y Patrick Leonard, mientras que la producción estuvo a cargo de ambos junto con William Orbit.
-La canción contiene sonidos pop, dance, tribal, electrónico.
-Es considerada como una balada electrónica oscura.
-“Frozen” fue el sencillo líder del disco “Ray of light”.
-La canción logró posicionarse en charts de distintos países en el número 1, y en “Billborad hot 100” llegó a la posición número 2. 
-La canción “My Heart Will Go On” (banda sonora de “Titanic”) de Céline Dion evitó que "Frozen" alcanzará la primera posición en la lista de “Billboard hot 100”.
-En la canción “Frozen ” Se nota una clara evolución en el rango vocal de Madonna, debido a que anteriormente en el año 1996 protagonizó la película “Evita”, en la que se preparó en vocalización con expertos en la materia.
-El videoclip “Frozen” se filmó en el desierto de Mojave, en California, bajo la dirección de Chris Cunningham.
-El vídeo está inspirado en la película "El paciente inglés" de 1996, dirigida por Anthony Minghella. Así como en los trabajos de la bailarina Martha Graham y el autor Edgar Allan Poe.
-Madonna introdujo un alter ego llamado "Verónica Electrónica", una encarnación espiritual.
-El videoclip “Frozen” proyecta un estilo gótico y místico.
-Fue ambientado en un desierto, pues Madonna opinó que el desierto tiene muchos poderes místicos y es un lugar mágico para estar, además de que le inspiraba desolación y espiritualidad.
-Madonna luce un vestido negro largo y las manos cubiertas de tatuajes de alheña y el símbolo “Om” (que significa: unidad con lo supremo, la combinación de lo físico con lo espiritual) en una de sus palmas. A lo largo del vídeo, se transforma en una bandada de cuervos y en un perro negro.
-Madonna demostró su profesionalismo durante la grabación del videoclip “Frozen”. Ella estuvo descalza en el desierto, soportando climas extremos (mucho calor de día y mucho frío por la noche). Se dice que también estuvo lloviendo en días de la grabación del videoclip.
-En el año 2000 para una entrevista de MTV Latinoamérica, Madonna confesó que “Frozen” había sido el video musical más difícil de hacer de toda su carrera. Pero valió el esfuerzo, porque es uno de sus mejores vídeos.
-En los MTV Video Music Awards del año 1998, Madonna recibió el premio a "mejores efectos especiales".
-La canción y el vídeo han envejecido increíblemente bien. Su ambientación y sonido suenan tan actuales. 
-"Frozen” es el claro ejemplo de que muchas canciones de Madonna se disfrutan mejor con el tiempo, como los buenos vinos.

content: Me
I like this one to.🩰

Over 15 years ago, I lived in rural Western Australia. Even for non Americans, the song American Pie  has a special place in the pop culture history of a generation.  The local TV channel often ran movies from 12 noon until 2pm. Many movies finished early. So they used short documentary and interview segments to fill in the 5 to 20 minutes spare time.  Usually taken from a series of "behind the scenes" type series of interviews.  One such interview I saw, but have never managed to find again, was with Madonna. In it she details how at first she outright refused to do "American Pie" (her reason was something along the lines of "That's Don's classic, it would be unfair of me to...etc"). However in the interview she described how over time her thinking changed and she chose to do her cover version of American Pie because ("insert reason I forgot, here").   The interview was beautiful and I recall being impressed by the good honour of Madonna's attitude.  If anyone ever finds THAT interview then please post a link to it here, so I'll hear about it and can see it again. cheers

2011: FAMILY GUY
Heck ya!!!!
This song stayed in my head for two weeks in September of 1989. Thirty years after, I did a drum cover of it and had fun doing it.

And I heard it when it was released. I was a teenager in the 80s . I swear what I’m about to tell you is true that, and I swear on everything I hold precious to me, that before Madonna was even known by the general public and had only released one single on a 45rpm when me and my best friend, who both loved music and were regularly in record stores checking out records and looking for new and intriguing singles that were released. We’d occasionally buy a single that we knew nothing about just because it looked cool to us and was in the music section we loved the most (at the time) . One day in 1982 we were in a cool record shop in Berkeley. We went to Berkeley regularly once we were 15/16 as it was only a quick train ride away.  Whilst  in this record shop we were both flipping through the singles section in different sections and I came across this record and was instantly fascinated. There was this  cool af young woman on the sleeve of the record dressed in the coolest clothes and accessories I’d seen, her style was so original when she started. Nobody wore crucifixes with fishnets and knee length trousers/pants and styled their hair like Madonna at the time. I called my friend over to show her the record I’d found and she completely agreed with me. We thought she looked like one of the coolest chicks we’d ever seen. So we bought the single and played it once we were back home. We were as equally impressed with the song as we were with her image. Within 6 months she started gaining public interest and then she released‘Borderline’ (I think) , she definitely released one of her first huge hits. None of our friends really believed we knew about her before she blew up, but we did. 
Maybe you don’t think that’s a cool story but I hope someone does or understands how ahead of the times with music and fashion that me and my best friend were, but we were! We were both 15 at the time!

2021 and soon 2022! 😉

2005: Time goes by so slowly
2020: Time goes by so quickly

At its release, this sound was underwhelming. Pop was full of harmonies, full sounds, drops, electronic beats. We’ve now had 6 years of minimalistic rnb, so a song like this that fell flat 10 years ago suddenly sounds fuller. Another reason is that this song is heavily influenced by work that wasn’t relevant in the 2010s. Nowadays, we long for the past a lot more (a byproduct of the pandemic and tiktok), hence why a song like this, that some described as “dated on release” now actually holds value.

Madonna is also a victim of mysoginy and blatant sexism / ageism. She was also at a weird turning point in her career where people expected her to lay down and turn into a legacy act, so her releasing something like this that could be seen as pandering to the younger demographic was seen as desperate. The pop scene in general was a lot more toxic back then still. Subsequently, Madonna was never going to be given a chance for a song like this, no matter the sound.

Ranma en Japones  Upon release, the video was criticized for its violent content. Music channels MTV and VH1 said that it would not be added to their regular US rotation and would air it just once, during a news segment, before being completely banned.[81] This was Madonna's third video to be banned by the channel, following "Justify My Love" (1990) and "Erotica" (1992).[80] "What It Feels Like for a Girl" aired on March 20, 2001, at 11:30 pm, following an introduction by MTV News anchor Kurt Loder.[78] Liz Rosenberg, Madonna's publicist, released a statement urging the channels "to make a very strong commitment to playing this video [...] there are many other possibilities that we can explore but our first choice has always been for VH1 and MTV to play [the video], and more than once".[82] In 2002, the video was aired in its entirety on MTV2 as part of a special countdown of the year's most controversial videos.[83]

Ce qui est bon est intemporel. 
A l'époque, ce clip a fait scandale. Le pape lui-même avait du Vatican émis une protestation timide devant la bronca des catholiques fervents qui fustigeaient Madonna à chacune de ses apparitions tant elle tordait le cou aux valeurs traditionalistes, mélangeant sexe et religion avec la plus naturelle décontraction. Pourtant, quand on le regarde aujourd'hui, on voit bien plus clairement que ce clip est tout sauf choquant, vulgaire ou équivoque, par comparaison avec ce qu'on voit maintenant, où, le plus souvent, l'image des femmes est dégradée presque systématiquement, signe évident que tout reste à faire pour qu'on lui donne enfin sa place d'égal à égal avec les hommes. Et c'est un mec qui le dit. Madonna avait l'intelligence de sa beauté, et vice-versa, mais sans jamais dépasser les limites de la décence. Clairement, elle avait et a toujours eu une longueur d'avance sur toutes ses concurrentes directes, et ce sur trois générations. 
Sa carrière prend fin sur une note un peu plus triste dirions-nous, puisqu'elle aussi a été transformée par cette adulation longue et exceptionnelle du public, des médias (pas toujours) et du milieu professionnel (tout le temps). Il ne faudrait pas vieillir...

Madonna didn't just do pop. She defined it and then redefined it. MADONNA= POP.  Both her voice and her music catalog are great. One has to have both to have hits across 4 decades. She laid a solid foundation from the very beginning. "Music makes the world go around"; "let love shine, and you will find a way to make things better"; "express yourself, so you can respect yourself". This woman had a vision, a beautiful voice and a fantastic determination to set her own marks and evolve. She has matured but she still has her girlish campy innocent sexiness. I think it has to do with her attitude towards life. She is never gonna give up her microphone, platform, bustiers and dancing shoes. Why should she??? That is who she is and why we love her. No disrespect to anyone, but there are plenty of 20 year old beautiful models. However they are not Madonna the multi talented artist who gave us so many timeless tracks, videos, styles and looks.
This woman is a musical institution/ dimension/ league of her own.

@DartMort A lot of people had parents like that unfortunately,but the majority of my childhood peers in the 70s and 80s appeared to me to be not cold and distant like that. Standards,attitudes and mores were different to today on many topics,and a lot things were taboo among the broad sweep of the public that are talked about more openly today, I was born in late 1964,which makes me one of the very last Baby Boomers according to some and one of the very first Gen Xers according to others. My parents were born just before the Second World War,so the generation before the Baby Boomers,and they were always warm and supportive to us,and we shared jokes and stories and anecdotes and could talk with them person-to-person about pretty much anything and they'd always be as positive as possible and make suggestions that were intended to help,whether we thought them suitable or not. They read us plenty of bedtime stories when we were little,from The Hobbit to Narnia to many more. They'd always be taking us out with them on trips,treats,activities and holidays. My Mum would still cuddle me sometimes at 16 and 17,a time of wanting to be an adult and treated as such the bulk of the time,but also still being in a lot of ways a child. Not that I didn't keep a few of my teenhood misdemeanours secret from them,but that was more because I didn't want to upset them than any possible consequences. Yes,they did have certain standards and expectations,and there were a few rules and boundaries. Sometimes we were told off,but not very often and typically in a constructive way that showed us why what we were doing was wrong. The household always had a relaxed atmosphere,though,and their friends would come and stay and we always had lodgers who would socialise with us too.They liked a bit of the same new music as me so we could always talk about that even if we didn't agree on a lot of things,and their record collection was my first introduction to a lot of enduring 60s and 70s favourites from the Kinks to Fleetwood Mac,Procol Harum to. My Mum kept up with modern music trends more than my Dad. She played piano and violin and is steeped in classic music but likes some folk,soft rock and even a bit of punk and New Wave,but was never keen on disco music; he loved his trad jazz but also classical and some of the then newer music,but little from any more recent than the 70s and early 80s appealed to him much. Madonna was already a little controversial with some for a couple of years before this but that seemed to be about cavorting in the clothes she wore,that looks wholly uncontroversial now. My Dad made some comment when seeing one of her videos on Top of the Pops for either Lucky Star or Like a Virgin,that began "well,if that's a Madonna,then..." but I can't remember the rest of it now.

Day! I was in a disco when this was out, the girl who lived in my street,who was just to die for, somehow fancied me!! She was there, in front of me, I was older than her, she didn’t give a shit, she danced in front of me, in her denims, I was scared, she wasn’t! I took her hand, walked her home, she was incredible, witty, beautiful, so sexy I couldn’t believe my luck. I messed her about as she was too good for me, also a little younger, though any bloke would have died for this beautiful creature! Not me, she was out of my league, according to me. I messed it up even though she still wanted me big time! One day, a friend of mine called me, he said he was gonna bring her up to mine as she wanted to talk & get back together, I said “ok”, they were outside of my house, I bottled it & his until they got bored & left, she was in tears, I messed up so much but my own persona of myself told me it was the right thing to do. Many years later, with her in my head all day every day, I got a text off my sister, telling me that Nicola had took her own life, aged 41. I live every day with the thought that I should have embraced this fabulous,funny,beautiful creature. Never ever suffer with low self esteem, it’s ruined my life, don’t ever let it get in the way of yours xx

A lot of ageist young people diss Madonna, but as someone who has kept up w/ pop music for over 50 yrs, there is no female artist in the music industry today who can compete w/ her.  Taylor Swift, Beyonce & Rhianna would probably come the closest, but I just don't think that any of them is the chameleon that Madonna is, nor as controversial or just flat out as interesting.  She isn't a great vocalist, but her taste in music & her instincts for theatrics are incomparable.  At my age, you have cycled though so many ephemeral flashes in the pan that you know who is worthy of real respect.  And w/o Madonna unleashing female sexuality in pop music back in the day, who knows what dreary state that female artistry  in would be in today.  She took a lot of arrows that enabled younger artists, gay & straight, to be as expressive as they are now.  Even by today's progressive sexual standards, "Erotica" & "Justify My Love" would raise eyebrows.  She's already a legend, & when she passes, she will be deified.

content: @romukimsawa5844  amazing

I'm from Russia, and in our country, this song was popping in the summer of 2008, to say the least. It was literally everywhere; radio stations and local music channels were playing it on the loop over and over again. Even my mom, who is not familiar with Madonna's discography, knows this song cause it was so huge back in the days. When I went to Madame x tour, I called my best friend who grew up in the same period as me and told her that I'm about to see Madonna. She is not a  fan of her, and just like my mom knows only a handful of songs from her catalog.  So,  she was like, «Madonna? Give it to me! YEAH. OMG, is she gonna sing it?» 😂 So, yeah. Even though this song was never a hit in the US, it was pretty successful in other parts of the world

content: @Carmen G Bro I understand what u were saying. Obviously I was not there to witness her fame but I know how much of a megastar she is judging by album sales, amount of crowds in her concerts, love, hits etc. But Britney was also the biggest artist of the 2000s she was in her own right a megastar. Her debut album sold 10 million by the end of 1999, oops I did it again sold 9 million by the end of 2000, Britney and in the zone both sold 5 million by the end of 2002 and 2004, making her have 4 albums that sold internationally above 5 million copies in their debut year and not many artist are able to do that. Her massive tours would always sell a MILLION tickets with high grossing and sold out tour dates. She would always have the most controversial and talked about performance of a award show she would attend and perform in. Her hits like baby one more time, oops I did it again, slave 4 u and toxic are huge parts of pop culture that are still sang to this day. Also Britney dolls were always sold and anything Britney related would be so. She had the best selling perfume of 2004 or 2005 when she released the curious perfume. She was always in the tabloids no matter what she would do and fascinated heavily by the media and general public. Let’s not forget that she was known as Rolling Stones most powerful celebrity in 2002. She was the first act in first time to have 4 of her albums sell above 500k and had consecutive 4 no1. Albums and already accumulated 6 by the end of 2009. Her 6th record, circus already went platinum status just after a month of its release. My strongest point is that she was the MOST SEARCHED person from the period of 1999-2008 and 2009 ended that run because of the death of the king of pop. There’s a reason her second record had the highest first week sales by a woman (1 million+) and the first by a woman to reach above a million copies in the United States. Her business ventures (besides the launch of her restaurant) were always successful and would by very high. She carried 7 HUGE hits by the end of 2000 and that’s a lot. Let’s not forget that she was arguably the most controversial artist of the 2000s and was also the best selling artist of the 2000s shifting over a 90 or 100 million record sales by the end of 2009.Britney had the fastest selling female album in 2000 and was highly popular. If you go to any britney video, you will see comments about how much she was a huge star bacl then. Besides the sales of her and being the biggest force in the music industry since Mariah Carey, she helped revive pop in a rock/hip hop dominated industry with few others and was the only solo artist at that time that wasn’t in a band and was successful. Her records like in the zone and blackout are highly critically acclaimed records and blackout having a place in the rock and hall fame with legends like Beatles etc. In the period of 1998-2008, it was a full on Britney mania.

content:
@misa95 (Gaga's little monster ♥) If Madonna flop everyone flopped... I mean, she is the most successful hits artist on Global Charts ever! (Elvis Presley comes 2nd).

Madonna sold over 75 million albums during the 80's decade and over 90 million albums during the 90's decade. 
As now, 40 years after she started her album sales are over 300 million units and way over 500 million records including singles, maxi-singles and EP's (probably even over 600 millions already including digital songs and streams equialents songs and albums)! 

Madonna was #01 Artist of the 80's in both US Hot 100 and UK Official Charts! During the 90's she still did pretty good in US and amanzingly well in UK being among the TOP 10 Most Successful Female artists in both countries. In the 00's decade Madonna slowed down a bit in US due to lack of airplay but in other countries, like the UK still got 15 TOP 10 Hits and 5 #01 singles during the 00's. Still despite not doing so well in US in the 00's Madonna still was best selling singles artists in US during the 2000's decade according to Billboard decade end issue (which is quite amazing). And before I forget Madonna was also a huge album seller too in both 80's and 90's.
 
Madonna still holds the record for female artist with most TOP 10 Hits in US and UK (38 in US including 12 #01 hits and 63 in UK including 13 #01 hits). In UK she even have a record 36 consecutive TOP 10 Hits (from '84 to '94) and 68 consecutive TOP 20 Hits (all her singles from 1984 until 2008 went TOP 20 - "Miles Away" peaked #37 in UK becoming her 69 consecutive TOP 40 and "Celebration" in 2009 peaked #03 to be her 70th and last consecutive TOP 40 Hit before "Revolver" missed the TOP 100 and break her string of TOP 40 Hits - another record Madonna holds in UK charts). In US Madonna scored a record 16 consecutive TOP 5 Hits from '84 to '89 (a record she still holds and this song "Open Your Heart" was a #01 hit on the Hot 100 back in 1986/1987 being part of that string).

Let's not forget also that Madonna is the Most Grossing Solo Artist in the World and also have the best selling Greatest Hits Album by a Solo Artist Worldwide.

Madonna most recent studio album was #01 on Billboard TOP 200 back in 2019. Her new "Frozen remix" from 2022 already charted on Germany, Swisstzerland and France Oficial TOP 100 Singles (in France was #64 this week after 6 weeks on the chart). In US the song is charting at the Hot Dance Songs for 19 weeks already (and peaked #10 just last week). After 40 years doing Music Madonna is still able to do this? How can anyone call her flop EVER!

I get that this song that's 36 years old may not sound "fresh" to you in 2022 but that doesn't mean it wasn't fresh when it was out and that was not a hit. You better educate yourself a bit more about Madonna and her work before posting jealous comments.

A humble suggestion to Madonna:
Album name: ‘Zephyr in the Sky’
An appeal by the fans to release a compilation of 20 masterpieces from Madonna’s catalogue personifying every emotion we have been through in this most dark age. And as we steer back to humanity and life. Search for the future.
A journey to the light, there is always a ray of hope and then the brilliant radiance of life.
Emotions and these songs clutch in perpetuity, the journey we continue.
 
 
Ray of Light
The deliverance of hope. The rhythm of life never seizes. There will always be that zephyr in the sky each night. Embrace it we can.
 
Masterpiece
Preservation of our most precious in peril. Our children, our people. Our kind. Caress them, protect them, enliven them.
 
Crazy
Souls without direction. Souls succumbing to the anarchy. Moments of lapse and dissolution of reason. There is always a method in any madness.
 
Miles Away
Separation - a reality, distancing in the new remote ecosystem – a way of life. Now from a far. Life anew.
 
4 Minutes
There were heroes. And there were those who saved the world, those who ultimately will and those who arrogantly don’t. We can all be heroes, for even more than a day.
 
Borderline
Barriers, walls, borders can neither prevent the triumphant human, nor conquer the invisible enemy. Humanity on the borderline of distraught. Never destroyed.
 
Die Another Day
Death can never be normalized. Not one, not a million. Fight. Death will come, in victory there will ultimately be death. But another day, its not our time to go.
 
You’ll See
Strength is personal, strength is contagion, strength prevails, strength is now our constant. You’ll see.
 
La Isla Bonita
My dreams are always my own and even I don’t control them. There will be sun again, there will be celebration again, we will hold hands again –into that beautiful sunset.
 
Ghost Town
The cradles of our civilization – devoid of voices, people, laughter, love. Ghostly in silence, our great cities shall live again – thrive they shall.
 
Like a Prayer
God. Life is a mystery, everyone must stand alone. You will hear the voice and I always hear you call my name. Forgive me. Pray.
 
Joan of Arc

Vogue

Human Nature

I rise

I’ll Remember
I’ll remember the love. I’ll remember the love you gave me. Now that we’re standing on our own – we shall walk again together, we shall celebrate again together. Another circus, another show.
Music

In gratitude, Madonna.

content:
People today don't realize exactly how big of a deal this video was when it came out. At the time, Madonna was pretty much the biggest star in the world, maybe tied with Michael Jackson, but even he was a bit past his prime. She was it. And when this video was released, literally hell broke loose. It was immediately banned by MTV (still an extremely relevant channel at the time), and the controversy was all anyone could talk about. It was released on VHS tape -- the first song to be released as a 'video single', I think. She was on 60 Minutes, she was on every magazine cover, and Tipper Gore was all over it. And really, compared to everything that had come before it, this video was extremely explicit. Nobody had seen a major pop star behave like this before. I was 13, and my parents wouldn't let me watch it at all. I of course found a way, but it was like nothing I had ever seen.

Nowadays we have constant access to basically any kind of video you can imagine, including every variety of pornography in existence. We barely even think twice about seeing a celebrity or pop star naked or even on a sex tape. But in 1990, it was unheard of to see the biggest star in the world perform sexually like this. Madonna deserves a lot of credit for doing it tastefully. 

Now she’s a lunatic alien plastic surgery zombie, unfortunately, but there was a time when she was extremely sharp and relevant.

content: Let's celebrate the 25th (!!!) anniversary of Secret single release. Beautiful international comeback single! After poor commercial reception to her 1993/early 1994 releases internationally the ballad Secret brought back Madonna to worldwide popularity! To coincident with promotion of upcoming Bedtime Stories album Madonna left the message to audience in INTERNET  (!!!)  what was something new and unusual in 90s (we can basically say That Madonna invented web audio messages and podcasts back in 94 😮😮). The song became the biggest hit from the album internationally, peaking in top 10 in almost every country it charted. The Secret got to number 3 on US Hot 100 in November 1994. It also recived huge radio airplay across the US and world, fans were very often requesting the song from radio DJ to be played. Happy 25 birthday SECRET 😙😙😙
